name: apifier
description: Scrape and ingest external API documentation (OpenAPI, Postman, AsyncAPI, GraphQL, gRPC, HTML, Markdown) into a portable apifier-mapping.json file; generate language-specific client modules from a mapping; validate and self-check.

tools.name: apifier-scrape
tools.description: Ingest API documentation from a URL, local file, or pasted spec text and write an apifier-mapping.json file. Auto-detects source type (OpenAPI 3.x, Swagger 2.0, Postman Collection v2.1, AsyncAPI 2.x, GraphQL SDL/introspection, .proto, raw HTML/Markdown). Honors robots.txt by default. Returns the output_path and a parse-summary (endpoint count, warnings, source provenance).

source.description: URL (http/https), absolute file path, or inline spec text. Type is auto-detected; force with source_type.

service_name.description: Slug used for the output filename. Defaults to a slug derived from the spec's title or hostname.

output_dir.description: Absolute path. Must be inside the allowed-roots list. Defaults to ~/.orchestray/apifier/mappings/.

render_js.description: Enable headless rendering (Playwright) for JS-heavy HTML sites. Requires playwright peer-dep; off by default.

tools.name: apifier-list
tools.description: List apifier-mapping.json files under the mappings directory. Returns metadata only (no full mapping bodies), suitable for piping to selectors or UI listing.

include_invalid.description: Include files that fail current-schema validation.

tools.name: apifier-generate
tools.description: Emit a language-specific client module from an apifier-mapping.json file. Live targets in v0.2.0: ts-fetch (byte-deterministic TypeScript fetch client — Wave 4A), python-requests (Python 3.8+ requests client — Wave 4B), openapi-3.1 (round-trip OpenAPI 3.1 YAML — Wave 4C), go-net-http (Go stdlib net/http client — Wave 4D). Planned: ts-axios, python-httpx (Wave 4E); curl-shell (Wave 6+). Unsupported targets return a structured CodegenNotSupportedError with the scheduled wave.

mapping_path.description: Absolute path to an apifier-mapping.json file.

target.description: Codegen target. Live: ts-fetch, python-requests. Others throw CodegenNotSupportedError.

out_path.description: Absolute path to write the generated module. Must be inside an allowed root.

tools.name: apifier-validate
tools.description: Re-validate an apifier-mapping.json file against the current schema. Reports schema_version, structural errors (missing required fields, invalid enum values), and migration suggestions when an older schema_version is detected.

tools.name: apifier-doctor
tools.description: Health-check the plugin install. Verifies node version, presence of required peer modules, mappings directory is writable, no quarantined files, and that every existing mapping passes current-schema validation.

dir.description: Mappings dir to scan; defaults to ~/.orchestray/apifier/mappings/.

tools.name: apifier-diff
tools.description: Compare two apifier-mapping.json files and report the SemVer impact of the change (breaking / non_breaking / patch). Useful for CI gates that block PRs introducing breaking API changes. Categorises additions, removals, and modifications across endpoints, parameters, responses, models, and auth schemes.

mapping_a.description: Absolute path to the BASELINE mapping (the 'old' version).

mapping_b.description: Absolute path to the CANDIDATE mapping (the 'new' version).

format.description: structured = full ChangeReport JSON. summary = top-line counts + verdict.

tools.name: apifier-watch
tools.description: Re-scrape an API doc source and diff the result against a committed baseline mapping. Returns the SemVer-classified change report PLUS a should_block boolean derived from a configurable severity threshold. Designed for CI gates: 'block PR if upstream API changes break our pinned baseline'. Atomic operation — fresh scrape + diff + verdict in one call.

source.description: URL, absolute file path, or inline spec text. Same accepted shape as apifier-scrape.

baseline_path.description: Absolute path to the committed baseline apifier-mapping.json.

service_name.description: Slug used for the freshly-scraped mapping filename. Defaults to a slug derived from the spec title or URL hostname.

output_dir.description: Where to write the fresh scrape. Defaults to ~/.orchestray/apifier/mappings/. Must be inside the allowed-roots list.

block_on.description: Severity threshold at which should_block becomes true. 'breaking' blocks only major verdicts; 'minor' also blocks minor verdicts; 'patch' blocks any change; 'none' never blocks (report-only).
